---

##    20\. Why You Should Approach Every Photo Shoot With No Expectations (feat. Kaitlin) 

Kaitlin teaches us to why we should walk into every photo shoot with NO expectations.

[ Complete and Continue   ]()

Discussion 

#### Post a comment

![Ashris Choudhury](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/4bc5e792ce4895a9603d9acf2dbb895e?d=mm) Instructor 

Ashris Choudhury

now

Your comment was posted. 

__Your comment was posted, but it needs to be approved by the school owner before it shows up.

"Comments have a maximum of 10000 characters."

![Add Image](https://fedora.teachablecdn.com/assets/icon-image-add-2bb59d5f21dbca28cca9cf9e6530fc84f468b86806ca92da9e2bdafa2aa398d0.svg)




####  25 comments 

![Keith M Hilgenfeldt](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/0152c6a6db02d12ef96b08c640ffb1fa?d=mm&s=70)

Keith M Hilgenfeldt

Awaiting Review 

2 years ago 

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/8598951/comments/23926937)

I totally agree with you, I used to always overthink a shoot and it never quite made an impact. When I started to have no expectations and went with the flow and allowed it to go in a cool direction, It always made the subject more free and made me relax as well. Amazing things come from this way of shooting, its why I really love street photography because it forces you into the moment and I have applied that to more elaborate shoots as well.

![Ashris Choudhury](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/4bc5e792ce4895a9603d9acf2dbb895e?d=mm)

Ashris Choudhury

DD Corbeau

Awaiting Review 

5 years ago 

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/8598951/comments/12802034)

“I only want to get naked for a picture, if it’s Art” —What the model feels —What the audience feels and what Art demands: that all participants (artist, subjects and audience) are made vulnerable Wow. She nailed it.

Haha... I like that ! The point of LNPTV is to give you an injection of inspiration. Sometimes it needs to be short to deliver that. I'm working on making it as close as possible to a pill form!

What comes through here for me is "let your muse have a voice" you don't work with them because they're silent and just sit and stare into space, you work with them because of who they are in that moment.
